spec_match.rationale: Info panel uses anchor-size() in `margin-left` (margin), `top` (inset via anchor()), and `min-height` (sizing — pre-131 only). Demonstrates the combined sizing + margin + inset pattern the feature unlocks.

interactive.rationale: Sidebar width slider + add/remove item buttons + per-item click handler + live computed readout. Strongly interactive.

blurb_alignment.rationale: Feature card promises a sidebar that the info panel mirrors via combined sizing and margin anchor-size() usage; page delivers.

edge_cases.rationale: Has a real @supports fallback with a visible fallback notice, gracefully handles empty selection state, disables remove-button at minimum item count, and updates the readout on ResizeObserver.

design_system.rationale: CSS variables, no raw hex, status-badge variants all set both background and color explicitly. One concern: the bare `button { background: var(--text-black); color: var(--bg-ivory); }` declaration RE-DECLARES the global button rule rather than relying on it — fine, but should use `:where(button)` or a `.btn` class for specificity reasons. The `.secondary` button override sets `background: var(--bg-ivory); color: var(--text-black);` together — passes rule #6.

openQuestions.title: Demonstrate the height-mirror direction too
openQuestions.detail: The min-height: anchor-size(height) only matters when the sidebar is taller than the panel content. With the default 5 items the panel naturally fits — visitor never sees the mirror engage. Add a 'minimise panel content' toggle that hides the description so the visitor sees min-height kicking in.

openQuestions.title: Localise the bare `button` selector
openQuestions.detail: The inline stylesheet redeclares `button { ... }` globally for this page. If a future inline JS injects a third-party button (e.g. analytics consent) it would inherit these styles. Move to `.demo-btn` or use `:where(button)`.

openQuestions.title: Add a readout row for panel.min-height
openQuestions.detail: The current readout shows sidebar width, sidebar height, and computed margin-left. Add `panel min-height` so the visitor can confirm anchor-size() in min-height is doing what the code claims.

summary: Strongest concept in the feature — combines all three spec capabilities (sizing, margin, inset) with a real @supports fallback, live readout, ResizeObserver, and interactive items. Just polish suggestions.
